raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citibank 's handling of a disputed credit card change. 
Amount : {$250.00} Transaction Date : XX/XX/XXXX Statement Period : XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX This was an online transaction that I disputed on XX/XX/XXXX after realizing I had mistakenly submitted payment to the wrong rental company. 
During the investigation, the merchant provided as evidence a lease agreement signed in XX/XX/XXXX. 
That lease : 1. Predates my relationship with the person who signed it 2. Expired several years before the XX/XX/XXXX transaction 3. Does not reference my credit card 4. Does not demonstrate authorization for any transaction in XX/XX/XXXX Despite this, Citibank ruled against me on XX/XX/XXXX. 
I reopened the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, specifically explaining that the lease does not establish authorization of this transaction. I was not contacted during the reinvestigation. On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank closed the case stating that the time limit had passed and that no further action would be taken. 
The statement containing the charge covered XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. I disputed the charge well within 60 days of that statement. 
Citibank has not explained how a XXXX expired lease agreement constitutes authorization for a XX/XX/XXXX online XXXX transaction XXXX 
I am requesting that Citibank : 1. Conduct a proper reinvestigation of this billing error.

2. Provide a written explanation detailing how the documentation relied upon establishes authorization of this specific transaction.

3. Correct the account if authorization can not be demonstrated. 
I believe Citibank improperly relied on irrelevant documentation and then closed the reinvestigation on procedural grounds without addressing the substantive issue of authorization. 
I have submitted a formal written billing error notice under the Fair Credit Billing Act to Citibank 's designated Billing Inquiries address.
